text_blocks.text: Many of the farms in the Western
Cape province in South Africa still bear French
names. Many families, today mostly Afrikaans-speaking,
have surnames indicating their French Huguenot
ancestry. Examples include: Blignaut, Cilliers, de Klerk
(Le Clercq), de Villiers, du Plessis, Du Preez (Des
Pres), du Randt (Durand), du Toit, Duwenhage(Du
Buscagne), le Roux, Lombard, Malan, Malherbe,
Visagic (Visage). The wine industry in South Africa
owes a significant debt to the Huguenots, some of
whom had vineyards in France, or were brandy
distillers, and used their skills in their new home.
